_instructions: Manually reviewed against official vendor documentation on 2026-07-11. Agent snapshots preserve pre-correction values; truth uses the current strict production-access rubric and exact canonical auth sets.
access_rubric: Self-Serve only when a new developer can obtain credentials usable in production without manual approval, partnership, business verification, or already being a paying customer. A sandbox or trial alone is not production Self-Serve; otherwise use Gated.
selection: Forty-five reviewer-priority apps across every catalog category; each new batch excludes all prior human and cloud-browser verification sets.
method: Manual review of first-party API, authentication, production-access, pricing, and MCP documentation. Community MCP ownership is labeled only when no vendor-owned server was found.

truth.notes: Connect REST uses OAuth and the Salesforce DX MCP is vendor-owned. API access is enabled in paid production editions; the free Developer Edition is documented as a development/testing environment before moving changes to production, so the strict production rubric is Gated.

correction.access_note: Free Developer Edition supports development and testing; production API access requires a supported paid edition or API add-on.
correction.main_blocker: Production API access requires an existing customer on a supported paid edition or an API access add-on.

truth.notes: HubSpot documents OAuth and independently issued service keys sent as Bearer credentials. A standard account can be free and is explicitly the production environment. HubSpot publishes its own remote MCP server.
Slack Web API apps are installed with OAuth 2.0 and receive access tokens. A developer can create an app in a workspace without vendor approval. Slack hosts the official MCP server.

GitHub REST accepts self-created personal access tokens and OAuth or GitHub App user authorization. Token creation is self-serve. The github/github-mcp-server repository identifies itself as GitHub's official MCP server.

Notion documents OAuth for public connections and personal access tokens for user-scoped API access. It does not document a separate API-key credential for the REST API. Workspace owners can create PATs on the free plan. Notion MCP is hosted by Notion.

Shopify's primary Admin and Storefront surfaces are GraphQL. API calls use OAuth-derived Admin tokens or independently issued Storefront access tokens; a client API key is not accepted as the API credential. Custom distribution is available without App Store review. Shopify owns Storefront MCP.

Stripe REST accepts secret or restricted API keys and Connect supports OAuth. Stripe explicitly requires business verification before services can be used on a live account, so test keys alone do not make production access Self-Serve under the rubric. Stripe hosts its MCP server.

correction.access_note: Test keys are immediate, but live Stripe services require business verification and account activation.
correction.main_blocker: Production use requires Stripe business verification and activation of the applicable live service.

truth.notes: Jira Cloud REST accepts a self-created API token through Basic transport and supports OAuth 2.0 3LO. Basic Auth is not an independent credential in this case, so the exact credential set is API Key plus OAuth2. Sharing an unreviewed 3LO app is allowed with a warning. Atlassian Rovo MCP supports Jira.

Google Ads requires OAuth 2.0 plus a developer token on every API call and documents both authorized-user and service-account workflows. Developer-token applications can be restricted to test access or require review for broader production levels. Google publishes the MCP server guide.

WhatsApp Cloud API uses permanent system-user access tokens and Facebook Login for Business onboarding. Tech-provider production access requires business verification and App Review. The checked MCP server is owned by a community GitHub account, not Meta.
Attio documents OAuth 2.0, workspace API keys, and optional HTTP Basic authentication. Production workspace keys are self-serve, and Attio hosts the MCP server.

Freshdesk authenticates an API key through HTTP Basic. The free plan has zero API calls and the trial is temporary, so production API access requires a paid plan. Freshdesk documents its vendor MCP integration.

correction.access_note: Freshdesk Free currently permits zero API calls; production API use requires a paid Growth, Pro, or Enterprise account.
correction.main_blocker: Production API credentials require an existing paid Freshdesk customer account.

truth.notes: Front supports bearer API tokens and OAuth. API quotas are attached to paid Starter, Professional, or Enterprise plans, so production access requires a paying customer. Front hosts an official MCP server.

correction.access_note: Front API access is included with paid plans; the self-serve trial is temporary and does not satisfy the production-access rubric.
correction.main_blocker: Production API use requires an existing paid Front customer account.

truth.notes: The HTTP Bot API uses the single bot token issued self-serve by BotFather. API ID and hash belong to the separate MTProto surface and are not another REST Bot API auth method. The cited MCP is community-owned.

Meta Marketing API uses user or system-user access tokens obtained through Meta authorization. Limited access is development-only; production apps need Full Access through App Review. No vendor-owned MCP was identified; the catalog entry is community-owned.
LinkedIn Marketing APIs use three-legged OAuth. Advertising API access requires an application and production Standard tier requires further review. The identified MCP package is community-owned.
Klaviyo documents private API keys and OAuth for server-side APIs. Credentials can be created from a developer account, and Klaviyo publishes the official MCP server.

WooCommerce generates consumer API keys and sends them with HTTP Basic over HTTPS. Store owners can create keys without vendor approval, and WooCommerce includes native MCP support in developer preview.

SP-API is REST and uses Login with Amazon OAuth 2.0 tokens. Production requires a professional seller/vendor relationship, registration, roles, and approval. No Amazon-owned SP-API MCP is documented; the previously cited AWS Partner Central MCP serves a different product.
Vercel supports account access tokens and OAuth authorization for integrations. Tokens are self-created in account settings, and Vercel hosts the official MCP server.
The Management API documents personal access tokens and OAuth 2.0. Both are available without manual approval, and Supabase hosts the official MCP server.
Datadog REST uses API and application keys; MCP additionally supports user PATs and service-account SATs. Keys are account-managed, and Datadog publishes the official MCP server.
monday.com exposes GraphQL with personal tokens and OAuth for apps. Members can retrieve tokens without vendor review, and monday.com hosts the official MCP endpoint.

Plaid APIs use client ID and secret credentials; OAuth is used for supported institution flows and its MCP token flow. Production requires Plaid enablement or approval, and Plaid hosts the Dashboard MCP server.
Fathom explicitly documents API-key, bearer-token, and OAuth 2.0 SDK authentication. API keys are generated in settings, and Fathom hosts the official MCP server.

Zoho CRM V8 uses OAuth 2.0 access tokens; client IDs are OAuth client credentials, not an independent API-key auth scheme. The Free edition has production API credits, and Zoho publishes its MCP servers.
Close REST supports API keys over HTTP Basic and OAuth 2.0. Production accounts are paid after the temporary trial, and Close hosts the official MCP endpoint.

correction.access_note: Close provides a temporary trial; continued production API and MCP use requires a paid Close subscription.
correction.main_blocker: Production credentials require an existing paid customer account.

truth.notes: Copper documents OAuth and X-PW-AccessToken API-key authentication. The account becomes paid after its trial, so production credentials require a customer subscription. The identified MCP is third-party.

correction.access_note: Copper provides a temporary trial; continued production API use requires a paid Copper subscription.
correction.main_blocker: Production credentials require an existing paid customer account.

truth.notes: DealCloud REST supports authorization tokens and API keys inside an existing licensed environment. Production access is customer-gated, and DealCloud now documents its own MCP server.
Help Scout Inbox API uses OAuth 2.0 while its Docs API uses an API key over HTTP Basic. Production access requires a paid account after the trial. The identified MCP is community-owned.

correction.access_note: Help Scout provides a temporary trial; continued production API use requires an active paid Help Scout account.
correction.main_blocker: Production credentials require an existing paid customer account.

truth.notes: Gorgias private apps use API keys over Basic Auth and public apps use OAuth 2.0. Only a short trial is free, so production requires a paid plan. Gorgias documents its MCP integration.

correction.access_note: Gorgias provides a seven-day trial; continued production API and MCP use requires a paid plan.
correction.main_blocker: Production credentials require an existing paid customer account.

truth.notes: Plain GraphQL uses workspace API keys and the official MCP uses OAuth. The seven-day trial is temporary and all production plans are paid.

correction.access_note: Plain provides a seven-day trial; production API and MCP use requires a paid Foundation, Horizon, or Frontier workspace.
correction.main_blocker: Production credentials require an existing paid customer account.

truth.notes: Aircall REST supports Basic Auth for customer credentials and OAuth for partner apps. API access is attached to a paid Aircall plan, so production is customer-gated. The identified MCP is community-owned.

correction.access_note: Aircall API access is bundled with paid Aircall plans and therefore requires an existing customer account.
correction.main_blocker: Production credentials require an existing paid customer account.

truth.notes: Vonage APIs span API-key/secret Basic auth, JWT bearer credentials, and OAuth-based network APIs. Credentials are issued from the self-serve developer dashboard, and Vonage publishes official MCP servers.
HighLevel platform APIs use OAuth 2.0 or a private integration token sent as Bearer. API access starts on paid agency plans after the trial. HighLevel hosts the official MCP endpoint.

correction.access_note: HighLevel API access is available on paid agency plans after the temporary trial.
correction.main_blocker: Production credentials require an existing paid customer account.

truth.notes: Pinterest API uses OAuth 2.0. Trial access is development-only and Standard production access requires a reviewed upgrade with a video demonstration. The identified MCP is community-owned.

SendGrid uses API keys through Bearer or supported Basic authentication. New free access is a time-limited trial, so continuing production use requires a paid plan. The identified MCP is community-owned.

correction.access_note: New SendGrid accounts receive a time-limited trial; continuing production email API use requires a paid plan.
correction.main_blocker: Production credentials require an existing paid customer account.

truth.notes: Magento Open Source and Adobe Commerce support bearer tokens and OAuth-family flows. PaaS integrations use OAuth 1.0a, represented as Other Token because the locked vocabulary has no OAuth1 label; Cloud Service supports OAuth2. Magento Open Source is self-hostable, and the identified MCP is community-owned.
Squarespace Commerce APIs support OAuth and API keys. API keys require an eligible paid Commerce plan and OAuth clients require review. The identified MCP is community-owned.
Ahrefs supports API credentials and OAuth through Ahrefs Connect. Production API and MCP use require a paid Ahrefs subscription, and Ahrefs publishes the MCP server.
Apify REST authenticates with a self-created API token, canonicalized as API Key. A free account can run production actors within its limits, and Apify publishes the official MCP server.
Netlify REST supports personal access tokens and OAuth integrations. Tokens are self-created on free accounts, and the netlify-owned repository provides the official MCP server.

Snowflake REST supports OAuth, programmatic access tokens, and key-pair JWT authentication; the latter is canonicalized as Service Account rather than Other Token. Production requires a paid Snowflake account, and Snowflake publishes official MCP tooling.

Sentry supports personal tokens, OAuth 2.0, and a distinct static internal-integration authentication token represented as Other Token. Free production projects are available, and the getsentry organization owns the official MCP server.

Coda REST and MCP use user-generated personal access tokens, while MCP also supports OAuth2 with PKCE. Coda explicitly makes the API available to free workspaces and publishes the official MCP endpoint.
